comment: Galaxy-spin handedness counts (clockwise vs counter-clockwise relative to the Milky Way). Shamir 2025 (MNRAS 538 76) reports a ~50% excess of one rotation direction in JWST/JADES; the two leading interpretations are (i) a universe 'born rotating' (black-hole-cosmology / parent-spin inheritance) and (ii) a Milky-Way-rotation aberration systematic. Galaxy Zoo (Land+2008) earlier found consistency with isotropy. CAUTION: a raw count asymmetry is a MONOPOLE; an observer/MW effect is a DIPOLE -- separating them needs sky-resolved counts NOT encoded here.

tfpt_expectation.note: TFPT carries only a mu4 clock + PSL(2,C)~SO+(3,1) boundary orientation -> at most a TINY global orientation remnant, NOT a ~20% handedness excess. So TFPT predicts approximate parity; a large robust asymmetry is NOT a clean TFPT prediction (the parent-spin-inheritance reading is frontier/conditional).

datasets.name: Shamir 2025 JWST/JADES
datasets.note: 263 galaxies with identifiable rotation; ~50% more counter-clockwise

datasets.name: Galaxy Zoo (Land+2008)
datasets.verdict: consistent with isotropy after correcting human-vote bias (no significant handedness)

systematics: Milky-Way rotation aberration (a DIPOLE; Shamir notes correcting for it may explain the excess)
annotation / mirror-image labelling bias (Galaxy Zoo lesson)
selection + viewing-angle effects at high z
